CHAPTER 542 .
AN ACT to extend the boundary of the cityof Owensboro .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky:
§ 1. That theboundariesof the cityof Owensboro shall
be as follows,viz: Beginning on the Ohio riverwhere the
streetbetween Shelby and N. B. Allen comes to the river;
thence south along the west lineof saidstreetin a straight
line,to theroadthatleadstoMonarch'sdistillery; thence
down that road to the line between Morton's and A. More-
land's; thence south along said line to the Henderson
road ; thence up the north margin of said road to thejunc-
tion of Walnut and McFarland streets; thence south along
the east side of avenue or road leadingout to Ray's,to
the divisionline between Thos .Higgins and Jas. Weir ;
thence east along that line,and continuing in that direc-
tion,to the Hartford road ; thence acrossthe same ,contin-
uing thesame course,one hundred and fiftyyards; thence
towards the Ohio river,parallelwith the Hartford road, to
the Litchfield road ; thence east with the course of said
road to the old Litchfieldroad ; thence down the sarne,on
the west margin ,to the river.
§ 2. But itisfullyunderstood and provided that none of
the lots or territoryso added to the present limitsof the
cityof Owensboro shallbe subjectto,or liableto,thecity
railroadtax leviedby the authoritiesof said cityto pay its
subscriptionto the Owensboro and RussellvilleRailroad,
nor to pay the intereston bonds issuedtherefor,forthree
(3) years afterthe passage of thisbill.
§ 3. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 13, 1872.

tees of said town shallhave power to regulatethe laying
off the streetsand alleys and lotsin the said extension,
and personsowning lands in said extension,when they
desiretolay offlotstherein,shallconform to the platlaid
down by the said trustees; but nothing herein shall be
so construed as to compel the owners of land within
said extensionto laythe same offintolots, nor to pre-
vent them from selling,using,and controlingtheirprop-
ertyfor agriculturalpurposes. The trustees, or other
officersof said town , shallhave no power to fax the

CHAPTER 554 .
AN ACT to prohibitthe saleof spirituousliquorsin the town of Antioch,
Metcalfecounty
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky:
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for the county court of
Metcalfe county,or any person, to grant licenseto any
person or persons to sellspirituous,vinous,or malt liquors
within two miles of James Withers' mill,in said county ,
or in thetown of Antioch; and any personwho shallsell,
give ,or loan spirituous,vinous,or malt liquorswithin the
districtnamed in thisact,shallbe deemed guiltyof keep-
ing a tippling-house,and shallbe finedin any court of
competent jurisdictionas provided by law .
§ 2. This act shalltakeeffectfrom itspassage,and be
in forcefor fiveyears, and no longer.
Approved March 14, 1872.

CHAPTER 567 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Maysville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the dutiesimposed upon the city collector
shall, afterthisact takeseffect,devolveupon the citytreasurerand citymarshal as follows,viz: Upon the city
treasurerup tothe firstThursday in September,and after
that time upon the citymarshal ,each year ; and the re-
spective bonds of the treasurer and marshal shall cover
the dutiesherein imposed,and shallbe forsuch amounts

30 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1872 . as thecitycouncilmay require: Provided, That thisact
shallnot,in anywise,affectthe powers, duties, and re-
sponsibilitiesof the collectorelectedfor the year 1872 , or
any collectorthatmay be appointedby thecouncilincase
of his removal ,death,or resignation: And providedfurther,
That if the treasureror marshal failor refuseto give the
bond or bonds requiredforthe collectionand payment of
the taxes,the council shall have power to remove them ,
oreitherof them ,and appoint a treasureror marshal in
theirstead,who willexecute the requiredbond .
§ 2. That afterthisact takes effect, the officeof city
collector, as heretofore existing, shall cease, and all the
dutiesand powers conferredby law and the ordinancesof
the cityon the collectorshallbe ,and the same are hereby ,
conferredon the treasurerand marshal,respectively, as
herein setforth. All special assessments shallbe placed
in the hands of the marshal for collection,and he shallbe ,
except as limited by the collectionsof the treasurer,the
collectorof the city.
§3 . That the cityclerk shall place the annual assess-
ment and tax lists in the hands of the treasurer as he is
now required to place the same in the hands of the col-
lector, takingthe treasurer'sreceiptfor the same , and
charging him with the amount ; and the treasurer shall
proceed tocollectthe taxeswithoutdelay in the manner
prescribedby law inregard to thecollectorup tothe first
Thursday in September,when he shallsettleup,and at
once place alltaxes uncollectedby him ,on that day ,in the
hands ofthecitymarshal,takingduplicatereceiptsforthe
same ,one forhimself and the other to be returned to the
city clerk; and it shallbe the duty of the marshal , by
himself and deputies, to collectand account for all the
uncollected taxes thus placed in his hands ; and the mar-
shalshall,inallrespects,from thattime forward,perform
the duties of collector,and be accountable for the taxes
thus placed in his hands according to law . The board of
councilmen shallallow such compensation to the treasurer
and marshal, fortheirserviceshereunder, as theymay
deem reasonable .
§4 . This act to take effectand be in full force from
and afterthe firstday of January , 1873.
Approved March 18, 1872 .

ness,and to repeal,alter,or modify thisactat pleasure.

CHAPTER 605 .
AN ACT for the benefitof the suretiesof Jas. B. McWhorter ,late sheriff
of Taylor county .
WHEREAS , James B. McWhorter , sheriffof the county
of Taylor , for the years 1863, 1864 ,and 1865,failed to
pay into the State Treasury the several sums for which
he was liableas collectorof public revenue ; and whereas ,
his suretieson his bond as sheriffaccounted to the State
Treasurer for the balance unaccounted for by said Mc-
Whorter ; and whereas ,there are yet due and uncollected
taxesdue forthoseyears,which ought to have been paid
to said sheriffand accounted forby him to the Treasury ;
and whereas ,also,there are many fee billsdue said Mc-
Whorter , as sheriffaforesaid,yet uncollected, which sums
ought to go in liquidationof the sums paid by them as
surety aforesaid; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of the county of Taylor
shall appoint a collector,to whom allfee billsdue said
McWhorter ,as sheriffaforesaid, for the years aforesaid,
shall be delivered,which collectorshall have the neces-
sary power and authorityto collectall uncollected taxes
and fee billsdue said McWhorter , for the years afore-
said; and forwhich purpose he may have every lawful
power that officershave in the collectionof taxes and
fee bills; and shall,as a collectingofficer,be responsible
forany abuse of thispower .
§ 2. Before the collectorso appointedshallproceed to
execute his duties under this act,he shallbe required by
the county court to execute a covenant to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky,with good security, conditionedfor
the faithfulperformance of hisduties.
§3 . All the moneys collectedby saidcollectorshall,in
due time, be paid over to the suretiesof the said Mc-
Whorter on his bond for the collection of the revenue
for the years aforesaid, in the proportion that they, as
suretiesaforesaid,have paid intothe Treasury the sum
due by saidMcWhorter ,as suretyaforesaid.

sheriffsare forperforming similarservices.

common fenceshould be builtand kept up,the economy
of such a fence being manifest in the fact that seven
miles of fence would inclose some fifteen thousand acres
of land,the necessityexistingin the fact that when the
floodsoccur late in the spring itwould be impossible to
get in a crop,if the farms were to be firstfenced. It is
furtherbelieved that the proposed common fence can be
so located as to be made permanent ; and whereas ,the
cost of saidcommon fence should be shared alikeby those
interested; whereas ,said fence crosses two county roads
and gatesarealreadyestablished,no inconveniencethereby
resultingto thetravelingpublicfrom locationof said road ;
and whereas,the expense of buildingand keeping said

ting up stockinsideof said inclosure,and to preventthe
turning loose of stock before all parties shall be in a
conditionnot to be injuredthereby,and to regulatemany
and various other matters connected with the use of a
common fence,unnecessary to enumerate ; and whereas ,

be established; therefore,

1872 . CHAPTER 728 .
AN ACT to regulatethe ratesof tollon the Germantown and Bridgeville
Turnpike Road .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the following shallbe the rates of tollon the
Germantown and BridgevilleTurnpike Road forfivemiles
travel on said road,viz : Hogs ,per head ,one and a half
cents; sheep,per head ,one and a half cents; cattle,per
head , two and a half cents; horses or mules in droves of
ten head each ,three cents; horses or mules in droves less
than ten head each ,four cents; every head of four-footed
animals of the largest size, four cents; horse and rider,
fivecents; gig or buggy drawn by one horse,fifteencents;
family carriages or buggies drawn by two horses,twenty
cents; cartdrawn by one horse,fifteencents; cartdrawn
by two horses,twenty cents; spring wagon drawn by one
horse,fifteencents; spring wagon drawn by two horses,
twenty cents; wagon drawn by two horses,thirtycents;
wagon drawn by three horses, thirty-five cents; wagon
drawn by four horses,fortycents; wagon drawn by five
horses,fiftycents;wagon drawn by sixhorses,sixtycents;
sleighdrawn by one horse,ten cents; sleighdrawn by two
horses,twenty cents; allother vehiclesdrawn by horses or
oxen shallpass at the same rates.
§2 . That it shallbe lawful for the company to erect a
half-way gate at or near Matilda Case's residence on said
road .
§ 3. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 25, 1872.

CHAPTER 733 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the Fox Run and Lagrange Turnpike Road
Company , in Shelby county.
WHEREAS , The board of managers appointed in the
originalcharterof the Fox Run and Lagrange Turnpike
Road Company having failedtohold the annual election
forofficersof saidcompany on theday prescribedin said
charter; and whereas ,said board have continued to super-
intend the constructionof said road at the request of the
stockholdersof said company without a formal election;
and whereas ,doubts exist as to the legalityof their acts
forwant of such election; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the said board of managers are hereby con-
tinued in officeuntilthe regular annual electionof said
company ; and allthe actsof said board not inconsistent
with the originalcharterare hereby legalized.
§2 . This actshalltakeeffectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 25, 1872.

§1 . That the Union and Richwood Turnpike Road char-
ter be so amended that they may have the rightto charge
not exceeding the following ratesof toll: For each horse
and mule and rider,one centper mile; foreach jack,jen-
net,mule,or horse,ledordriven,threefifthsof a centper
mile ; for each buggy ,carriage,or sleigh, drawn by one
horse, two cents per mile; for each buggy , carriage, or
sleigh,drawn by two horses,threecentsper mile; foreach
spring-wagon , cart, or dray,drawn by one horse, three
cents per mile; for each spring-wagon , cart, or dray,
drawn by two horses,four cents per mile; for each jolt-
wagon ,drawn by one horse,three cents per mile; for each
jolt-wagon ,drawn by two horses,fivecentsper mile; for
each jolt-wagon ,drawn by threehorses,sixcentsper mile;
foreach jolt-wagon ,drawn by fourhorses,sevencentsper
mile ; for each jolt-wagon , drawn by five horses, eight
cents per mile; for each jolt-wagon ,drawn by sixhorses,
nine cents per mile; for each vehicle attached to, and
drawn behind another vehicle, one half of the above
charges will be made ; foreach head of sheep,one tenth
of a cent per mile; foreach head of hogs,one tenth of a
cent per mile ; foreach head of cattle,two fifthsof a cent
per mile; also,thatpersonscoming on said turnpikeroad
between Leasing'sand the Frogtown road,or John Baker's
lane,or at said points, willbe charged for one mile of
travel; personscoming on the turnpikeatthe intersection
of the road leading to Richwood Church with the turn-
pike,willbe chargedforone and one fourthmilestravel;
personscoming on the turnpikeat McAtee'sor Mrs.Emily
Utz's house ,will be charged one and three fourths miles
travel;personscoming on the turnpikeat R. D. Hedges '
gate,or between that point[and] Mrs. Emily Utz'sfarm,
will be charged two and one half miles travel; persons
coming on the turnpikeat F. Dickey's, willbe charged
three and one fourth miles travel .

: CHAPTER 741 .
AN ACT to amend chapter No. 315,of Acts of 1869-'70,entitled"An act
to incorporateRio Bridge Company ."
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That sectionfourth of said act of incorporationbe
amended so as to read as follows, to-wit : " They shall
hold their officeuntil the firstSaturday of April, 1871,
when an electionshallagain be heldat Rio Bridge (in-
stead of the words 'at said town ') for their successors;
and every firstSaturday in April in each year a new
electionshallbe held at said Bridge to fillsaid offices."
§2 . That sectionsixth of said act of incorporation be
so amended as to read as follows, to-wit : " Each loose
head of sheep, hogs, or goats, two cents" (insteadof
four cents).
3. That any person or persons who shall pass over
said bridge, and refuse to pay the tollschargeable to
them , shall be subject to a judgment for said tolls, and
also a fine of not less than one dollar nor more than five
dollarsand costs,recoverableby suit,in the name of “ Rio
Bridge Company ," before any justiceof the peace or
town or countyjudge; saiddebt,fine,and costto be col-
lectedby any sheriff,constable,or town marshal of Hart
county ,in likemanner as other debts and finesare col-
lected .
§ 4. Whereas ,the State of Kentucky is the owner of
stock to the amount of eight thousand dollars in said
bridge,and her saidinteresthas not heretoforebeen rep-
resented; the Governor of thisCommonwealth shallap-
point some discreet person ,who shall act as agent for,
and represent, the Statein allbusinesstransactionsof
saidcompany . Said agent shall unite with the president
of the board of directors in his annual report to the
Auditor of Public Accounts , as provided for in the act
of incorporation. He shallhold saidpositionas agent
untilremoved by the Governor .
§ 5. This act to be in forcefrom itspassage .
Approved March 25, 1872.

CHAPTER 746 .
AN ACT to incorporate the town of Paintsville.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§ 1. That the boundary of the town of Paintsville, in
Johnson county ,shallbe as follows,viz: Beginning at the
mouth of the firstravine on north side of Paint creek,
below the mill,including the bridge over it,and running
thence to a rock at the point of the hillbelow town ;
thence with the foot of the hill, including the house of
John W. How ,to the ford of Paint creek,known as the
Spradlin ford; thence down the creek,including it to the
high water-mark on the south side,to a point opposite
to the beginning point; thence across the creek to the
beginning .
§ 2. The suburban limitsof said town shall be as fol-
lows : beginningon Sandy river,at the head of Buffalo
Shoal ; thenceby top oftheridgetothe head of theMud-
dy branch ,includingallof said branch ; thence to the head
of A. W. Rule'sbranch ,including allof it; thence to the
branch opposite mouth of Jennie's creek,so far up it as
to include the house of A. J. Fox ; thence south to Paint
creek,so as to include all the houses opposite mouth of
Jennie'screek; from thence,includingthe house of Allen
Collins',to the head of Hencliff, and from thence to Sandy
river,at Hellsgate shoal,including all the houses on the
premises of F. M. Stafford; and from said shoal to the
beginning ,so as to include the houses at the mouth of
Buffalo .

CHAPTER 749 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Cloverport and Hardinsburg Turn-
pike Road Company ,in Breckinridgecounty.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the board of directorsof the Hardinsburg and
CloverportTurnpike Road Company are hereby author-
ized,if they deem itexpedient,to issuethe bonds of said
company to any amount not exceeding seven thousand
dollars($7,000), bearing interestnot to exceed ten per
centum per annum ,theinterestto be paid semi-annually.
Said bonds shall be made payable in not exceeding ten
years from theirdate ; but may be redeemed sooner at the
pleasure of the board of directorsof said company .
§ 2. Said bonds shallbe signed by the president,and
countersignedby the secretary,and shallhave the force
and effectof mortgage bonds ; and to secure their pay-
ment ,allthe property and franchisesof the said company
shallstandpledged; and iflegalproceedingsareinstituted
toenforcethepayment ofany bond authorizedtobe issued
by thisact,such proceedingshallbe forthe benefitof all
the holders of said bonds ; and the presidentshallmake a
correctlistof allthe bonds which may at the time be out-
standing,and verifyitby his affidavit,which affidavitand
listshall be filedin the Breckinridge circuitcourt,upon
which court exclusivejurisdictionis hereby conferred for
such enforcement .
§3 . This actshalltakeeffectfrom itspassage.
Approved March 25,1872.

ers,and under allthe liabilitiesand responsibilitiesto her-

quired by law .

CHAPTER 751 .
AN ACT to amend an act to authorizethe Shelby county court to issue
bonds forthe benefitof turnpike roads.
WHEREAS , Doubts existas to whether the county court of
Shelby has the power and authorityunder said act to em-
brace county turnpike roads already constructed and in
debt to pay off said debts and aid said roads in keeping
them in repair; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That said act be so amended as to authorize the
county court of Shelby county ,a majority of all the jus-
ticesof saidcounty concurring,at any regular meeting of
the courtof claims in said county ,if they think proper, to
issuebonds of saidcounty,as providedin saidact,toaid,
to the extentthey may think just and right,any turnpike
road company in said county now in debt forthe construc-
tion of their road : Provided, That thisamendment shall
not apply to any road which has receivedthe benefitof
said act .
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 25, 1872.

Railroad Company .

Evansville,Henderson,and NashvilleRailroadCompany ,
and touse the proceedsof such saleor salesin payment
of the indebtednessof said cityor county ,eitherdue or to

cilfor same shall concur therein by a vote at a regular
meeting of said council; and no such saleshall be valid
and bindingupon the part of saidcounty unlessa ma-
jorityof two thirdsof allthe justicesof the peace in and
forsaid county shallconcur thereinby a regularvote when

sage .

CHAPTER 769 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof John Moore , jailerof Green county.
WHEREAS , ItappearsthatJosephTennison,a confirmed
lunatic,has been confined in the county jail of Green
county from the 24th day of February, 1871, up to the
24th day of February , 1872 ; and that said lunatic was
ordered to be confined in said jailby order of the Green
circuitcourt, owing to the fact that said lunatic could
not be received into any Asylum of the State for the
want of room ; and that John Moore ,the jailerof Green
county ,has faithfullyprovided for and taken care of said
lunatic; therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly ofthe Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§1 . That the Auditor of Public Accounts be , and he is
hereby ,authorized and directed to draw his warrant on
the Treasury ,in favor of said John Moore ,for the sum of
two hundred and seventy-four dollars and fiftycents, to
be paid out of any money in theTreasury not otherwise
appropriated.
§ 2. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 25,1872 .

CHAPTER 772 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Lewis Plummer ,jailerof Lewis county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1. That theAuditor of PublicAccounts isherebydi-
rected to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer of the
State in favor of Lewis Plummer ,jailerof Lewis county ,
for one hundred and fortydollars and twenty -fivecents
($ 140 25),tobe paid out of any money not otherwiseap-
propriated,itbeingin fullof the amount due said Plum-
mer forkeepingMariah McLane ,a lunatic,in the jailof
Lewis county,from June 19th, 1871,tothe3d day ofSep-
tember, 1871, and in fullfor keeping Lewis Kirk,a lu-
natic,in the jailof Lewis county,from the 28th day of
November ,1871,to March 20th,1872 .
§ 2. That said Lewis Plummer shallbe allowed the sum
of seventy-five cents per day for keeping said Lewis Kirk
from the20thday of March ,1872,untilhe isdischarged,
to be paid out of the State Treasury ; the claim to be al-
lowed by the Lewis circuitcourt,and certifiedas other

CHAPTER 773 .
AN ACT authorizingthe presidingjudge of the Breathittcounty court to
submit to the qualifiedvotersof said county, the proprietyof imposing
an ad valorem and capitationtax forthe constructionof a bridge across
the North Fork of the Kentucky rivernear the town of Jackson.
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That the presidingjudge of the Breathittcounty
court may , by order entered of record, at any regular
term of the Breathittcounty court,cause to be submitted
to the qualifiedvotersof said county ,at any regular elec-
tion,the question of imposing a tax of ten cents annu-
ally on each hundred dollarsof property assessed in said
county under the State assessment, and fiftycents on
each tithein said county ,until the sum of five thousand
dollarsisraised,to be held and used as a fund forbuilding
and constructing a bridge acrossthe North Fork of the
Kentucky river,nearthe town of Jackson,insaid county.§2 . Upon the order of said court the sheriffof said
county shallmake proclamationof at leastthirtydays
before the day appointed for said vote; and the clerk
of each electionprecinct shall open two columns upon
the poll-book ,in one of which he shall record the votes
" For the bridge tax," and in the other the votes"Against
the bridge tax," as now required by law ; and if a ma-
jorityof thosevoting,upon a comparison of the pollsby
those authorized by law to compare them , shallbe found
to have votedforsaidtax,thesame shallbe imposed,and
the sheriffof said county shallcollectitupon the same
terms and conditions as he now collects the State revenue .
§3 . The fund arising from said tax shall be held, and
faithfullyand exclusivelyappliedin thebuildingand con-
structionof said bridge, under the order and direction of
John H. Wells,William Day , John W. Cardwell, F. H.
Ketchum ,and Oliver Crawford ,who are hereby appoint-
ed commissioners forthatpurpose ,and who ,before receiv-
ing said fund,or any part thereof from said sheriff, shall
enter into bond and good security,in said county court,
forthe faithfulperformance and dischargeof allthe duties
imposed by thisact.§ 4. Said commissionersshallproceed to let out and
construct said bridge as soon as a fund sufficientfor the
constructionand completion thereofshallhave been raised.
§ 5. The presidingjudge of saidcountycourtshallap-
point one or more efficientpersons as commissioner or
commissioners ,whose duty it shallbe to settlewith said
bridge commissioners,from timeto time,and reporttheir
settlementsto said county court.
§ 6. This act to take effectand be in force from and after
itspassage .
1872 .
Approved March 26, 1872.

AN ACT forthe benefitof H. C. Thompson and W. T. Champion , of Liv-
ingston county.
-
WHEREAS , It is represented to this General Assembly
that H. C. Thompson is an infantof the age of years ;
that he has a family; and that he has an estatein the
hands of W. T. Champion ,his statutoryguardian,the in-
terestof which isnot sufficientto support said family;
therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That H. C. Thompson be ,and he ishereby ,relieved
of the disabilityof his infancy,and he is authorized and
empowered to settlewith and receiptto his said guardian ,
and any receiptor acquittanceexecuted and delivered by
the said H. C. Thompson ,or any settlement made by him
with his said guardian ,shall be as binding and as obliga-
tory on said infant as if he were of fullage .
§2 . That said H. C. Thompson may demand of said W.
T. Champion ,a settlementof his accounts,and pay the
same over to him .
§3 . This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage .
Approved March 26, 1872.

sage .

court at itsMarch term , 1869 .
WHEREAS , The General Assembly of Kentucky passed an
actwhich was approved March 9,1869,entitled"An act
regulating and changing the times of holding the quar-
terlyand countyand magistrates'courtsin thecounty of
Greenup," by which thespringterm ofthe quarterlycourt
of said county was changed from March to May ; and
whereas ,the judge of said quarterlycourt of Greenup , in
ignorance of the change that was made in the time of
holdinghiscourt,heldhiscourtin March insteadof May ;
therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That alljudgments and ordersmade and rendered
at the March term , 1869,of the Greenup quarterlycourt,
are hereby legalizedand made valid.
§ 2. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved March 28, 1872.

CHAPTER 902 .
AN ACT to prevent the sale of spirituousliquorsin the town of Burks-
ville .
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That from and after the time when this act takes
effectitshall not be lawful for the county court of Cum-
berland county,or thetrusteesof the town of Burksville,
or any other court or person,to grant a license to any
person or persons to sellwhisky, brandy, or any other
spirituous,vinous,or malt liquors,or the mixture of either,
in the town of Burksville,and within two miles of the
court-house of the same , nor shall it be lawful for any
tavern-keeper, merchant,distiller, druggist, or any other
personor persons,to sellany such liquorsin any quantity
less than a barrel,within the limits of said town , nor
within two miles of the court -house in said town .
§ 2. That if any person be convicted of a violation of
the foregoingsection,he or they,forthe firstoffense,shall
be finedfiftydollars, and one hundred dollarsfor each
succeedingoffense,to be recoveredupon an indictment
of a grand jury, or warrant beforethe policejudge of
the town of Burksville . The fines thus assessed and re-
covered to be collected as other fines .
§ 3. All acts inconsistentherewith are hereby repealed.
§ 4. This act shall take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
Approved March 28,1872.

not otherwise appropriated.

Approved March 28,1872 .

CHAPTER 927 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the Great Western Mining and Manu-
facturingCompany .
WHEREAS , The General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky ,by an actapproved January 19th,A. D. 1856,
created William P. Mellen, ArchibaldBorders, Laban T.
Moore , William T. Nicolls, William M. Snow ,and Daniel
A. Glidden ,their associates,survivors, successors,and as-
signs, a body-politicand corporate,by thename and style

430 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1872 . of " The Great Western Mining and Manufacturing Com-
pany," forthe purposeof mining coal,ironore,and other
minerals,and building,equipping,and maintaininga rail-
road or railroads; and whereas,itisjustand proper that
certain amendments should be made ,so as to remove all
doubtsas to thefunctionsand powers of saidcorporation;
now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneral Assembly of theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the said William P. Mellen ,and hisassociates
above named ,their survivors, associates, successors, and
assigns,incorporatedunder the above named act of the
General Assembly of Kentucky,may ,in accordance with
the power delegated to them in the fourth and fifthsec-
tionsof the actof incorporation,builda railroador rail-
roads, from theirlandor lands,to any point on the Ohio
riverat or near the mouth of the Big Sandy river, or to,
at,or near Pound Gap ,on theState linebetween Ken-
tuckyand Virginia; the said railroador railroadsto be
known as the Ohio, Big Sandy , and VirginiaRailway
Company ,with the rightto issuestockto an amount not
exceeding two million of dollars, in shares each of the
par value of one hundred dollars; and allthe other rights
and privilegesgranted to the said " The Great Western
Mining and Manufacturing Company ," in relation to
building, equipping, and maintaininga railroador rail-
roads,areherebyconferredon the said Ohio,Big Sandy ,
and Virginia Railway .
2. That the title of the said " The Great Western
Mining and ManufacturingCompany " be,and the same
ishereby,changed to " The Great Western Mining ,Man-
ufacturingand TransportationCompany ."
Approved March 28, 1872 .

CHAPTER 946 .

Louisvilleand Nashville Railroad, and there remains un-
paid about one hundred and fifteenthousand dollars of
the bonds of said county issued to aid in the construction
of said road ; and whereas ,by virtueof an act approved
February 10th, 1864,the people of said county have here-
toforevoted in favor of sellingsaid stock in order to pay
off said bonds ; and whereas , the county court of said
county , a majority of the justices in commission being
present,have requestedthe passage of an act authorizing
said court to sella sufficientamount of said stock to pay
the indebtedness of said county :

of Kentucky :

of the justicesof said county being present and concurring
therein,be,and they are hereby ,authorized to sella suffi-
cient amount of the stock of said county in the Louisville
and Nashville Railroad to redeem the outstanding bonds of
said county issued in aid of said railroad; and after re-
servinga sufficientamount of said stock,or itsproceeds,
to redeem saidbonds ,said court may sellthe remainder of
said stock and apply the proceeds thereof to the payment
of the debts of said county now existingor that may here-
after be contracted .

CHAPTER 972 .
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of ardent spiritsin Breathittand Powell
counties .
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1.That fromand afterthepassageof thisactitshall
not be lawfulforthecountycourtsof Breathittor Powell
counties,or any one ,to grant a licensetoany person tovend
ardent spiritsor malt liquorsin said counties of Breathitt
and Powell ; itshall also be unlawful for any merchant
or other person to vend ardent spiritsor malt liquors
in quantitieslessthan a barrel,within the limitsor juris-
diction of said counties of Breathitt and Powell ,except
for medical purposes upon the writtenprescriptionof a
practicingphysician.
§2. Any person violatingthe provisionsof thisact
shall be fined one hundred dollars for the firstoffense,
and two hundred dollars foreach succeeding offense, on
indictment of a grand jury.
§3 . The judge of the circuitcourt shallgive thisact in
charge to the grand jury.
§ 4. This act shall not take effect until it shall have
been ratifiedby a majority of allthe qualifiedvoters,who
shallvote at the August election,1872 ; and itshallbe the
duty of the county court clerksof Breathittand Powell
countiesto prepare two columns on the poll-books for
said election,one to be headed for prohibitionand the
other againstprohibition; and the officersof the elec-
tionshallcertifytheresultof saidvoteinthe same man-
ner as isnow prescribedby law forotherelections.
§5 . This act shall not interferewith the law as it now
stands in regard to distillers.
Approved March 28, 1872.

CHAPTER 990 .
AN ACT to regulateofficialsalesin Woodford county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
§1 . That allofficialsalesto be made by the sheriffor
master commissionerof Woodford county,of realor per-
sonalproperty within said county, shall,whenever said
propertymay exceed two hundred and fiftydollarsin
value,be advertised in four consecutive numbers of some

518 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
1872 . newspaper publishedin said county,or in an adjacent
county; in theeventof no such paper beingpublishedin
Woodford county,the costof such advertisingtobe paid
by the person authorizedto sellsaid propertyout of the
proceeds of such sale.
§2 . That the cost of such advertisement shallnot ex-
ceed the sum of ten dollars, when the value of the property
to be sold shall be lessthan one thousand dollars,nor shall
the costof advertising,as providedforin the firstsection
of this act,exceed twenty dollars,when the value of the
propertyto be soldshallexceed one thousanddollars.
§3 .All acts or parts of acts, localto the county of
Woodford ,inconsistentherewith ,are hereby repealed.
§ 4. Nothing hereinshallbe construedas repealingor
modifying the general law in relationto salesunder judg-
ments or orders of courts; and the failureto advertise, as
requiredby thisact,shall in nowise affectthe validityof
any sale, or be cause for settingaside or quashing the
same .
§ 5. This act to take effectfrom and after itspassage .
Approved March 28,1872 .
CHAPTER 991 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof W. S. Shepperd,of Pulaski county.
WHEREAS , A lunatic, by name of James Walters , was
orderedtobe conveyed totheEastern LunaticAsylum ,at
Lexington,Kentucky,by orderof the judge of thePulaski
circuitcourt; and the sheriffsummoned as a guard W. S.
Shepperd,who defrayedhisown expenses,and was refused
both pay for his servicesand expenses by the superin-
tendent,which amounted to twenty -fivedollarsand eigh-
ty-fivecents,allof which appearsby satisfactoryevidence;
therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
§ 1. That the Auditor of Public Accounts be ,and he is
hereby , directed to draw his warrant on the Treasurer
forthe sum of twenty-fivedollarsand eighty-fivecents,
in favor of W. S. Shepperd (for servicesand expenses
in conveying , as guard , said lunatic to the Asylum as
aforesaid),to be paid out of any money in the Treasury
not otherwise appropriated.
§2. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved March 28 , 1872 .

CHAPTER 992 .
AN ACT for the benefitof William Risner and Charles Menix ,suretiesof
StephenHoward ,sheriffof Magoffincounty.
WHEREAS , At the term , - , of the Franklin
circuitcourt,a judgment was rendered against William
Risner, Charles Menix , and others, suretiesof Stephen
Howard ,sheriffof Magoffin county,for a balanceof the
revenue due from Magoffin county to the State for the
year 1862 ; and whereas , execution was issued on said
judgment and placed in the hands ofthe sheriffof Magof-
fincounty,who ,under saidexecution,soldthe home farm
of William Risner,and the home farm of Charles Menix ,
in satisfactionof said execution; now ,therefore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky:
§ 1. That whenever William Risner or Charles Menix
shallpay intotheStateTreasurythe amount ofprincipal,
interest,and cost of the amount forwhich theirrespective
lands sold for,the amount of the damages on said execu-
tion shall,by virtueof thisact, be fullyand completely
remitted,and the said Risner and Menix discharged from
thepayment of such damages ,and the titleto theirsaid
lands revert and vest in the said Risner and Menix as
fullyas ifno salehad everbeen made of the same : Pro-
vided, That saidpayment shallbe made withintwo years
from the passage of thisact,with interestat the rate of
ten per centum per annum ,from thedate of saidsales,on
the amount the said lands sold for,lessdamages .
§ 2. This act shall take effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage .
Approved March 28, 1872.

CHAPTER 998 .
AN ACT to increasethe dutiesof the county attorneyof Barren county.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof theCommonwealth
of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in addition to the dutiesnow imposed by law
upon county attorneysin thisCommonwealth ,it shall be
the duty of the county attorney of Barren county to aid
the Commonwealth's attorneyin the prosecutionof all
persons in the circuitcourt of said county ,charged upon
indictment with a public offense: and he shallreceivefif-
teen per cent.of all fines,forfeitedbail bonds ,and for-
feited recognizances,obtained in saidcases,to be deducted
from the amount now allowed by law to the Common-
wealth's attorneyin said cases.
§ 2. That said attorneysshallhave no power to compro-
mise any of said cases in said county,but each shall be
tried by the court or by a jury;and if eitherof said attor-
neys shallso do,theyshallbe fined,upon indictmentin the
Barren circuitcourt ,in a sum of not less than one hundred
nor more than one thousand dollars .
§3 . This act shallbe in forcefrom the time of itspas-
sage forthreeyears.
Approved March 28, 1872 .

CHAPTER 1013 .
AN ACT to incorporateJ. M. S. McCorkle Lodge , No. 355, of A. Y. F. M.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyof the Commonwealth of
Kentucky :
§ 1. That the members of J. M. S. McCorkle Lodge ,
No. 355, of Free and Accepted Masons , in Brownsville,
Edmonson county,Kentucky,and thosehereafteradmitted
tomembership insaid lodge,be,and they are hereby,in-
corporated,in the name and styleof J. M. S. McCorkle
Lodge, No. 355, of Free and Accepted Masons ; and in
that name shallbe capable of contractingand being con-
tractedwith,suing and being sued, pleading and being
impleaded; and shallhave the power of acquiringand
holding,by purchaseor otherwise,realand personalprop-
erty, for Masonic purposes, not exceeding in value ten
thousand dollars,and may at any time selland convey the
same ,or any part thereof,and reinvestordisposeof the
proceeds;and to passand adopt such by-laws foritsgov-
ernment as itmay deem proper: Provided, The same be
not inconsistent with the laws and Constitution of the
United States or the State of Kentucky . The said lodge
may adopt a seal,and renew and break thesame at pleas-ure, That said lodge may build and erect,own and hold ,
or purchase,a hall,and such otherbuildingas they may
see fit,and receive, by donationor purchase, a burying-
ground ,if they see fit. That they may loan,or cause to
be loaned ,any moneys that they may have on hand ,and
collectthe same ,by suit or otherwise,in theircorporate
name ,or transactany otherbusinessthattheymay deem

proper and right; or may have power and authorityto in-
vesttheirmeans in bonds or stock,and hold or disposeof
them at pleasure.
§ 2. That in the three principalofficersof said lodge,
viz : the master and wardens ,as trustees,and their suc-
cessorsin office,arevestedfullpower to make allcontracts
pertainingtothe real and personalestateof said lodge,
which shallbe binding and obligatoryupon said lodge,
when made in pursuance to the rules, by-laws,and in-
structionsof saidlodge; thatisto say,thata majorityof
allthe members of said lodge concurring therein,and ser-
vice of process upon the master and wardens as trustees,
shallbe deemed sufficientnotice to said lodge. That the
General Assembly reserves the right to amend ,alter, or
repealthischarterat any time,upon therequestof a ma-
jorityof allof the members of saidlodge.
§3 .This act to take effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved March 29, 1872.

CHAPTER 1015 .
AN ACT for the benefitof J. N. Tague , F. E. Skidmore , W. Mahoney ,W.
B. Gillis, and M. Hunter ,suretiesof Jas.B. Cook ,latesheriffof Trimble
county .
WHEREAS , A judgment was rendered in the Franklin
circuitcourt on the firstJune, 1867, againstJames B.
Cook , late sheriffof Trimble county , for $ 4,819 35 , in-
cludingdamages and costs,and againsthissuretiesupon
his officialbond ; and whereas ,itappears that the said
suretiesabove named have sufferedtheirproperty, real
and personal,to be soldin satisfactionof saidjudgment ;
and that theyare now poor and unable topay more ,and
have no property,and ofwhich the sum can be made ,and
that a considerable sum yet remains unsatisfied; there-
fore,
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssemblyoftheCommonwealth
ofKentucky :
§1.That saidJ.N. Tague ,F. E. Skidmore,W. Mahoney ,
W. B. Gillis,and M. Hunter,arehereby releasedand dis-
charged from furtherliabilityon said judgment : Provided,
That nothing in thisact shallbe so construed as to in any
manner releaseJames B. Cook ,the latesheriff,from any
portion of said judgment .
§2 . This act shall not take effectuntil the said sure-
ties,who are stillheld by the provisionsof thisact for
the amount of said judgment ,shall give theirconsent to
same ,together with said James B. Cook ,latesheriff, & c.,
in open court,before the county court of Trimble county ,
which shall be certifiedto the Auditor of Public Accounts
by the county court clerk of said county .
Approved March 29, 1872 .
